items.title: (1/2) Last season, the Wizards' big-man rotation consisted primarily of Alex Sarr, Marvin Bagley III (before he was traded), Tristan Vukčević, Anthony Gill and whichever natural wing who wound up starting at the four (Khris Middleton or Justin Champagnie, for instance).
items.author: Josh Robbins

items.title: Alex Sarr underwent successful surgery today in New York City to repair a right foot fracture that he suffered during a workout that involved contact, Wizards officials said. The Wizards are confident that he'll be ready for the beginning of the season.
items.author: Josh Robbins
items.body_excerpt: Alex Sarr underwent successful surgery today in New York City to repair a right foot fracture that he suffered during a workout that involved contact, Wizards officials said. The Wizards are confident that he'll be ready for the beginning of the season.

items.title: Washington Wizards center Alex Sarr underwent surgery to repair a fractured right foot on Monday, sources tell ESPN. Sarr sustained the injury on a contact play during an offseason workout late last week. He is expected to be healthy for the start of the season.
items.author: Shams Charania Tweets
items.body_excerpt: Washington Wizards center Alex Sarr underwent surgery to repair a fractured right foot on Monday, sources tell ESPN. Sarr sustained the injury on a contact play during an offseason workout late last week. He is expected to be healthy for the start of the season.
